data.title: Avient reports Q4 sales up 2% to $761M, full-year sales up to $3.26B; Q4 GAAP EPS at $0.18, full-year at $0.89.
data.description: In the fourth quarter, sales saw a 2% rise, reaching $761 million, while for the full year, sales increased by 1% to $3,260 million. Foreign exchange positively influenced sales by 3% in the fourth quarter and 1% for the entire year. Comparing the fourth quarter results, GAAP EPS was $0.18, and for the full year, it stood at $0.89. This is a contrast to the previous year's $0.52 and $1.84 respectively.

data.title: TransUnion (TRU) beat estimated Q4 earnings by 10.31% with $1.07 EPS. Revenue increased $134M from last year.
data.description: TransUnion (NYSE: TRU) announced its Q4 earnings on Thursday, February 12, 2026, at 07:00 AM. In the report, they exceeded estimated earnings by 10.31%, revealing an EPS of $1.07 compared to the expected $0.97. Revenue showed a growth of $134.00 million from the previous year. Looking back, the company had surpassed EPS expectations by $0.11. For more details, the complete story can be found on Benzinga.com.

data.title: U.S. stock futures rose after strong January job report, adding 130,000 jobs, with 898,000 job revisions. Dow Jones up 0.21%, S&P 500 up 0.23%. Micron and Novocure in focus.
data.description: U.S. stock futures rose on Thursday after a decline, with major benchmark indices higher following a strong January jobs report: the U.S. economy added 130,000 jobs, surpassing expectations of 70,000 jobs, a significant improvement from December. However, sweeping benchmark revisions erased around 898,000 jobs from payroll estimates. On Wednesday, the Dow Jones index declined from a record-high close on Tuesday. The 10-year Treasury bond yield was at 4.175%, and the two-year bond yield at 3.502%. The Federal Reserve is likely to keep interest rates unchanged in March, with the Dow Jones up 0.21%, S&P 500 up 0.23%, Nasdaq 100 up 0.17%, and Russell 2000 up 0.39%. ETFs SPY and QQQ were up in premarket, with Micron (MU) and Novocure (NVCR) stocks in focus: MU rose 3.33% and NVCR soared nearly 33% in pre-market trading. Full story on Benzinga.com.

data.title: TransUnion reported strong Q4 2025 results with $1,171M revenue, $101M net income, and $0.52 diluted EPS. For the full year 2025, revenue was $4,576M, net income $455M, and $2.32 diluted EPS. They repurchased $300M of shares and raised dividend to $0.125 per share. They expect 8-9% revenue growth in 2026.
data.description: TransUnion (NYSE: TRU) announced financial results for Q4 2025, with revenue of $1,171 million, a 13% increase from Q4 2024, and a net income of $101 million. They repurchased about $150 million in shares in Q4 and $300 million in 2025. Their quarterly dividend increased to $0.125 per share. For the full year 2025, revenue was $4,576 million, up 9% from 2024, with a net income of $455 million. Adjusted EBITDA for 2025 was $1,646 million. They introduced 2026 guidance with an expected revenue growth of 8-9%. In the U.S. Markets segment, Financial Services and Emerging Verticals had revenue growth of 19% and 16%, respectively, in Q4 2025. Adjusted EBITDA was $417 million in Q4. Cash and equivalents were $854 million as of December 31, 2025. The company increased its borrowing capacity to $1.0 billion in February 2026. The outlook for Q1 2026 includes revenue of $1,195-$1,205 million and adjusted EBITDA of $414-$420 million.

data.title: Check Point Software Technologies stock rises after Q4 report. Revenue: $744.9M, EPS: $3.40 (beat $2.77 estimate). Subscriptions up by 11.26%.
data.description: Check Point Software Technologies Ltd. (NASDAQ: CHKP) saw a rise in stock value following the release of their fiscal fourth-quarter results. The Tel Aviv-headquartered cybersecurity provider reported quarterly revenue of $744.90 million, marking a 6% increase compared to the previous year. However, this figure fell slightly below the analyst consensus of $746.21 million. The company exceeded expectations in terms of EPS, with an adjusted EPS of $3.40 surpassing the anticipated $2.77. Revenue specifics showed growth in various segments: Products and licenses saw a 0.70% increase to $171.8 million, security subscriptions surged by 11.26% to $325.1 million, and software updates and maintenance rose by 2.95% to $248.0 million. Despite these positive numbers, the adjusted operating margin shrunk from 43.5% to 40.6% due to pricing pressures. For further details, the complete article can be accessed on the Benzinga website.

data.title: Avient beats estimated Q4 earnings by 3.7% with EPS of $0.56, up $14.10 million in revenue compared to last year.
data.description: Avient (NYSE: AVNT) released their Q4 earnings report on Thursday, February 12, 2026, at 06:15 AM. The results showed that Avient surpassed expected earnings by 3.7%, with an EPS of $0.56 compared to the estimated $0.54. Additionally, their revenue increased by $14.10 million from the previous year. In the previous quarter, Avient exceeded EPS expectations by $0.01, resulting in a 3.58% decrease in performance. More details can be found on Benzinga.com.

data.title: Keyera Corp. announces 2025 Q4 results with $1.13B adjusted EBITDA, $735M DCF, $432M net earnings, and strategic acquisitions.
data.description: Keyera Corp. announced their financial results for Q4 2025, with adjusted EBITDA at $301M and $1.13B for the full year, showing a decrease from the previous year. Distributable cash flow for the quarter was $206M, with a full-year figure of $735M. The company ended the quarter with net debt to adjusted EBITDA of 1.8 times, below the long-term target range. Keyera completed a strategic acquisition for $200M, and in early 2026, sold its interest in the Wildhorse Terminal for $65M. The company's growth projects are progressing on time and on budget, with capital expenditures for 2025 at $222M. Guidance for 2026 includes growth capital expenditures ranging between $400M and $475M, maintenance capital expenditures of $140M to $160M, and cash taxes decreasing to $60M to $70M on a stand-alone basis. The company reorganized its leadership structure in preparation for the acquisition of Plains. The full story is available on Benzinga.com.

data.title: PG&E Corp reported strong 2025 results: GAAP earnings $1.18/share, non-GAAP core EPS $1.50/share, tightened 2026 guidance to $1.64-$1.66/share, cut non-fuel O&M costs by 2.5% in 2025. PG&E made $2,593M income in 2025, $1.50 EPS, up from $1,236M income, $1.15 EPS in 2024.ANTAisEqualre above information.
data.description: PG&E Corporation has announced solid financial results for 2025 and has provided guidance for 2026. In 2025, GAAP earnings were $0.29 per share for the fourth quarter and $1.18 per share for the full year, compared to $0.30 and $1.15 in 2024, respectively. Non-GAAP core earnings were $0.36 and $1.50 per share for the same periods, showing improvement over 2024. The company has tightened its 2026 non-GAAP core EPS guidance to $1.64 to $1.66 per share. PG&E has focused on delivering safe, reliable, affordable, and clean energy to customers, including reducing residential electric rates by 11% since January 2024. The company achieved zero major wildfires caused by its equipment in 2025, completed various safety measures, and connected new customers and electric vehicle charging ports. PG&E Corporation plans to hold a conference call on February 12 to discuss these financial results. Public access to regulatory filings and other information is available on the company's website._ratios: PG&E Corporation recorded annual income of $2,593 million for 2025, or $1.18 per diluted share. Non-GAAP core earnings were $3,307 million, or $1.50 per diluted share, which excludes non-core items. Non-core items for 2025 were $714 million after tax. Operational data includes the completion of 334 miles of undergrounded powerlines and the installation of strengthened poles in high wildfire-risk areas. A focus on safety, reliability, and affordability continues to guide the company's decisions.
